name: Chocolate Raspberry Cake
recipeInstructions:
Cake: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease three 9-inch round cake pans and line with waxed paper. Dust with flour, shaking out excess.
Stir together the flour, 1/4 cup sugar, cocoa, salt, and baking powder.
In a large bowl, beat together egg yolks, 1/2 cup sugar, and melted butter until thick and pale yellow. Beat in vanilla.
In a medium bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Add remaining 1/4 cup sugar (1 tablespoon at a time) until stiff but not dry peaks form.
Sift 1/3 of the flour mixture over the egg yolk mixture. Add 1/3 of the egg whites and gently fold together. Repeat until all is mixed.
Divide batter equally among pans. Bake 15-18 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in centers comes out clean. Loosen cakes from sides with a thin knife, invert onto wire racks to cool completely, remove waxed paper.
Frosting: Melt chocolate and butter together in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring until smooth. Cool completely.
In a large bowl, beat remaining 1 1/4 cups heavy cream, powdered sugar, and vanilla until soft peaks begin to form. Do not overbeat or frosting will become grainy.
Fold 1/3 of the whipped cream into the chocolate mixture just until combined. Fold the chocolate mixture into the remaining whipped cream just until combined.
Assemble: Place one layer on a serving plate, spread with half the raspberry jam and about 2/3 cup frosting. Stack second layer, spreading with remaining jam and 2/3 cup frosting. Place third layer on top. Frost the top and sides with remaining jam and 2/3 cup frosting. Decorate with fresh raspberries if desired.
Refrigerate until ready to serve.
